channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Telehealth abortion will remain available for now, after a federal judge's ruling
item.description: The abortion pill mifepristone must undergo a safety review by the FDA, the judge said. Louisiana's case seeking to ban its use through telemedicine will proceed after that review.
item.content:encoded:
The abortion pill mifepristone must undergo a safety review by the FDA, the judge said. Louisiana's case seeking to ban its use through telemedicine will proceed after that review.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
item.description: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: The Artemis II crew saw parts of the moon never seen before. Here's what they said
item.description: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.
item.content:encoded: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
item.description: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.
item.content:encoded:
Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

(Image credit: Zayrha Rodriguez)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
item.description: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.
item.content:encoded:
In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: In a stark reversal, President Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran
item.description: President Trump has announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on their opening of the Strait of Hormuz. In an earlier online post, he had threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight."
item.content:encoded: President Trump has announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on their opening of the Strait of Hormuz. In an earlier online post, he had threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight."
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife
item.description: The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in 2020.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in 2020.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? Americans weigh options for saving gas
item.description: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.
item.content:encoded:
With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Photos: NASA releases first images from moon flyby
item.description: During the mission's loop around the moon, the crew took geological observations of places of interest on the lunar surface using their own eyes and snapping thousands of photos of the surface.
item.content:encoded: During the mission's loop around the moon, the crew took geological observations of places of interest on the lunar surface using their own eyes and snapping thousands of photos of the surface.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Australia charges ex‑soldier with 5 war‑crime murders in Afghanistan
item.description: Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.
item.content:encoded:
Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.

(Image credit: Anthony Devlin/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
